package handler

import "net/http"

// Positive scenario for domain_constants_declared_in_handler_package.
// Rule intent: Domain enums, statuses, and lifecycle constants that live in
// handler packages instead of domain-facing packages.

// These domain constants are declared in the handler package, which is the anti-pattern.
// They should live in a domain or model package.
const (
	StatusPending   = "pending"
	StatusActive    = "active"
	StatusSuspended = "suspended"
	StatusDeleted   = "deleted"

	RoleAdmin      = "admin"
	RoleEditor     = "editor"
	RoleViewer     = "viewer"
	RoleSuperAdmin = "super_admin"

	OrderStateCreated   = "created"
	OrderStateConfirmed = "confirmed"
	OrderStateShipped   = "shipped"
	OrderStateDelivered = "delivered"
	OrderStateCancelled = "cancelled"
)

type OrderHandler struct{}

func (h *OrderHandler) CreateOrder(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	status := StatusPending
	_ = status
}

func (h *OrderHandler) UpdateRole(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	role := RoleAdmin
	_ = role
}

func (h *OrderHandler) GetOrder(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	state := OrderStateCreated
	_ = state
}
